agent_analysis.hypothesis_rationale: Nonprofits with higher total expenses have higher overall budgets, allowing them to allocate more funds to fundraising activities (professional fees + event expenses).
agent_analysis.prior_art_notes: Weisbrod (1998) outlines scale effects on fundraising expenses.
agent_analysis.interpretation: A highly statistically significant but weak positive correlation was found. Larger nonprofits spend more on fundraising, but fundraising expenses make up a very small fraction of overall organizational spending.
